info.description: The Blockchain Management API provides endpoints to manage your (fuel) wallets and smart contracts. Besides that it also provides endpoints to retrieve transaction information.
info.title: Blockchain Management API

200.description: The user has a subscription for this asset

403.description: The user does not have a subscription for this asset

get.description: Checks whether the user has a subscription

items.description: the name of the chains that match the query

200.description: All supported public chains and all private chains from the client

get.summary: Autocomplete resource for chains

items.description: the name of the contracts that match the query

200.description: All contract names for the given chain from the client

get.summary: Autocomplete resource for contracts

200.description: All method fields for the given chain and contract from the client

get.summary: Autocomplete resource for contract methods

items.description: the name of the wallets that match the query

200.description: All wallet names for the given chain from the client

get.summary: Autocomplete resource for wallets

200.description: All supported public chains and all private chains (if any) from the client

get.description: First of all, this will return all the public chains we currently support at EnCo. If you have also created your own private chain(s) in the Blockchain-as-a-service API, these will also be returned. Last but not least you will also find the "EnCo shared" chain, which is a private Ethereum (POA) chain that we host and only available to EnCo users.
get.summary: Retrieve available chains

200.description: A list of smart contracts on the EnCo platform accessible by the requester

get.description: Call this endpoints to retrieve all contracts you deployed or imported on the EnCo platform.
get.summary: Retrieve all your contracts on a chain

200.description: Info about the deployed/imported contract

post.description:
You can use this resource to deploy a contract from scratch or import an existing contract.
The first option (deploy) is default when the "deploy" query param was not provided. You either have to provide the source code or the binary data & ABI.
If you want to import a contract, make sure to provide its contract address and the ABI or source code. Also set the "deploy" query param to 'false'

200.description: Contract deleted

delete.description:
Delete the specified contract from the EnCo platform. Please note that your contract will not be removed from the blockchain.
The deletion is definite and can't be reverted (unlike the wallet deletion, where it's possible to unarchive). If you want to add this contract to the EnCo platform again, you can always reimport it.

200.description: Contract from the client

get.description: Use this resource to retrieve info about a specifc contract you deployed/imported earlier.
get.summary: Retrieve a specific contract

200.description: Updated contract

put.description: You have the possibilty to make changes to a contract, at least on the EnCo platform as you can't change something on the blockchain. You can change the contract name, this won't have impact on the reference of the contract on the chain. If you decide to 'change' the contract code by providing new source code/data & ABI or import an existing one providing a contract address, we will change the reference to another contract (and address) on the chain.

parameters.description: The name or address of a wallet
parameters.name: walletIdentifier

200.description: Returns a gas estimation

400.description: Function arguments could not be parsed or do not correspond with a function's input arguments

500.description: Internal server error while invoking function

post.description: Call this resource to get an estimation of the fees you have to pay to invoke a certain function of a smart contract.
post.summary: Estimate the cost of calling a contract function

200.description: Swagger file describing endpoints to invoke this contract

500.description: Internal server error while providing swagger

get.description:
We at EnCo provide a way to invoke a smart contract function through our API.
Use this resource to generate the swagger file (describing all possible endpoints) for the specified contract.
get.summary: Create swagger for specified contract

parameters.description: The name or address of a wallet
parameters.name: walletIdentifier

200.description: Return value of the function invocation. If any.

202.description: Transaction hash of a non-view and non-pure function invocation

400.description: Function arguments could not be parsed or do not correspond with a function's input arguments

500.description: Internal server error while invoking function

post.description: Use this resource to invoke a smart contract function. Please provide the function arguments in the request body as a JSON where the field name is the argument name and the value is the argument value.

200.description: Returns info related to an address

get.description: Use this resource to retrieve address information. The address you have to provide can be either a normal (wallet) address or a contract address.
get.summary: Retrieve info about a specific address

200.description: Returns all transactions related to an address

get.description: Use this resource to retrieve all transactions related to an address. This could be a normal (wallet) address or a contract address.
get.summary: Retrieve info about transactions related to an address

200.description: Returns blocks based on optional start number and optional count

get.description: Use this resource to request information about certain blocks. You can specify which block(s) in the query parameters. If not query parameter is provided, the latest block will be returned.
get.summary: Retrieve info about certain blocks

200.description: Returns block based for block hash

get.description: Use this resource to request information about a specific block. Please make sure you provide the block hash, this is not the same as the block number.
get.summary: Retrieve info about a specific block

200.description: Returns all transactions in a block

get.description: Use this resource to request all transactions in a specific block. Please make sure you provide the block hash, this is not the same as the block number.
get.summary: Retrieve info about transactions in a specific block

200.description: Returns transactions that originated from your managed wallets or contracts

get.description: Use this resource to request information about the latest x transactions related to your managed wallets and contracts.
get.summary: Return info about transactions you made via the EnCo platform

200.description: Returns a transaction by its hash

get.description: Use this resource to retrieve transaction information. This could be a transfer, contract deploy or invocation.
get.summary: Retrieve info about a specific transaction

200.description: The block, transaction or address if one can be found

get.description:
Use this resource to retrieve information about a transaction, address (wallet or contract) or block.
Please provide the hash of these entities.
get.summary: Retrieve info about a transaction, address or block

200.description: The hash of the transaction where the wallet got funded

400.description: When a validation check failed for a certain faucet, a detailed errormessage will be returned.

404.description: When the provided wallet doesn't belong to the requester. Only wallets that are linked to your EnCo account are allowed.

get.description: To actually do something on a blockchain, you need funds. A lot of test chains provide a faucet to request funds. We at EnCo also created a faucet to fund your wallets on the "EnCo-shared chain". If you request funds on a public test chain (for example Rinkeby), we will provide you the URL of the faucet (if available).
get.summary: If you want to fund your wallet(s)

200.description: Wallets from the client on the specified chain

get.description: Use this endpoint to retrieve all your (EnCo) wallets on the specified chain. You can provide "balanceUnit" as query param to change the unit of "balance" in the response, the default value is WEI.
get.summary: Retrieve all your wallets on a chain

200.description: Created wallet

post.description: Use this endpoint to create a new (EnCo) wallet on the specified chain. You can give it a friendly name by passing the "name" query param, this name can be used as wallet identifier in other endpoints.
post.summary: If you want to create a new wallet

200.description: Archived wallets from the client

get.description: Use this endpoint to retrieve all your archived (EnCo) wallets on the specified chain.
get.summary: Retrieve all your archived wallets on a chain

200.description: Wallet successfully deleted

404.description: If the wallet could not be found or is not yours on the EnCo platform.

delete.description:
Use this endpoint to delete your wallet. Please note that this is a soft delete (archive).
You can still retrieve your archived wallets but you can't use archived wallets for other endpoint calls like transfers or exports.
If you do want to use an archived wallet, you first have to unarchive it at.

200.description: Info about the requested wallet

get.description: You can request info about a specific wallet by specifying the friendly name (only for wallets made on the EnCo platform) or the public address. It's not necessary to own the wallet on the EnCo platform to call this endpoint.
get.summary: Retrieve a specific wallet

200.description: Wallet renamed

400.description: If the wallet could not be found or the new name is already present.

put.description: Use this endpoint to rename your wallet. Please make sure this wallet is yours and made on the EnCo platform.

200.description: The exported walletfile and password

400.description: If the wallet could not be found or is not yours on the EnCo platform.

get.description: Use this resource to export your wallet to a walletfile and a password. With this file and password you can restore/reimport your wallet elsewhere.
get.summary: Export the walletfile and password

200.description: The public and private key pair

400.description: If the wallet could not be found or is not yours on the EnCo platform.

get.description: Use this resource to export the private key from the specified wallet. With this private key you can restore/reimport your wallet elsewhere.
get.summary: Export the private key

200.description: Transaction

post.description: Use this resource to make transfers.

200.description: Cancel a transaction as long as it is still in the transaction pool. When no gasLimit or gasPrice is provided, default values will be used.
OK

put.description: Update a transaction as long as it is still in the transaction pool, this information could be found in the block explorer. This resource will mostly be used to increase the gasprice and or gaslimit (fees) to make sure your transaction will be picked up faster. When no gasLimit or gasPrice is provided, default values will be used.

200.description: Unarchived wallet

404.description: If the wallet could not be found or is not yours on the EnCo platform.

put.description: If you want to make use of a deleted wallet again, you can unarchive it calling this endpoint. This is necessary if you want to use the wallet to make transfers, but also if you want to export the wallet itself.

address.description: An address or name of a wallet or contract
address.name: address

balanceUnit.description: The display unit of the balance
balanceUnit.name: balanceUnit

blockHash.description: A hex string representing a block hash
blockHash.name: blockHash

chainName.description: The name of the public or private chain
chainName.name: chainName

contracts.description: Whether or not to include transactions that originated from a contract. The default value is false
contracts.name: contracts

count.description: The number of blocks to return
count.name: count

deploy.description: Set this to false when the contract is already on the chain and you just want to import it to the EnCo platform
deploy.name: deploy

fundingAmount.description: The amount of ETH to send, must be a natural number [0-20]
fundingAmount.name: amount

hexString.description: A hex string representing a block hash, a transaction hash or an address
hexString.name: hexString

start.description: The number of the first block. If not provided, the latest blocks are returned
start.name: start

status.description: An optional filter to only retrieve transactions which are MINED, PENDING or QUEUED
status.name: status

transactionHash.description: A hex string representing a transaction hash
transactionHash.name: transactionHash

transactionId.description: The id of a transaction, also called transaction hash
transactionId.name: transactionId

walletIdentifier.description: The public address or name of the wallet
walletIdentifier.name: walletIdentifier

walletOrContractIdentifier.description: The address of a wallet or contract. This can also be the name of a managed wallet or contract
walletOrContractIdentifier.name: walletOrContractIdentifier

public.description: Whether the chain is public or private

contractDeployRequest.description: Must contain either source code OR abi & data. If solidity is given, data and ABI will always be overwritten.

contractAddress.description: Should be specified when the contract is already on the chain and the 'deploy' request param is false

contractDeployRequest.title: Contract deploy/import

password.description: The password to unlock to wallet file

functionArguments.description: Arguments needed to invoke the function.
functionArguments.title: Function arguments

name.description: The new name of the wallet

fromAddress.description: The public address sender of the transaction

gasLimit.description: The gas limit (in WEI) of the transaction

gasPrice.description: The gas price (in WEI) of the transaction

nonce.description: The transaction count (incremental) of the sender

toAddress.description: The public address recipient of the transaction

value.description: The value (in WEI) of the transaction

unit.description: Unit of the amount to transfer

transactionCount.description: Amount of transactions made by this wallet

tags.description: Endpoints to manage your wallets and make transfers
tags.name: Wallets

tags.description: Endpoints to manage your smart contracts and make invocations on them
tags.name: Contracts

tags.description: Endpoint to retrieve info about a certain transaction, address or block
tags.name: Block explorer

tags.description: Endpoints to support autocompletion on the chains, wallets and contracts for the requested user
tags.name: Autocomplete
